content_en:
Zakat, the obligatory annual almsgiving, is the third pillar of Islam and one of the most precisely regulated forms of worship. In al-Umm, al-Shafi'i addresses zakat with the same textual rigor he applies throughout his jurisprudence, specifying the minimum taxable threshold (nisab) for each type of zakatable wealth, the rate of zakat, the lunar year condition (hawl), and the valid categories of recipients.

For gold, the nisab is twenty dinars (approximately 85 grams in modern terms), and the rate is one-quarter of one-tenth (2.5%). For silver, the nisab is two hundred dirhams (approximately 595 grams), at the same rate of 2.5%. Al-Shafi'i holds that gold and silver may not be combined to reach the nisab — each must independently reach its own threshold. This contrasts with the Hanafi school, which permits combining gold and silver by converting them to a common value. Paper currency in later scholarship is generally analogized to silver in the Shafi'i school.

For livestock, al-Shafi'i follows the detailed schedule established by the Prophet's letters and zakah assessors. Camels are subject to zakat from five animals onward, with progressively scaled obligations as the number increases. The nisab for cattle is thirty heads, and for sheep and goats it is forty. Zakat on livestock applies only to animals that are freely grazing (sa'imah) for the majority of the year, meaning they feed themselves from open pasture rather than being fed at the owner's expense.

For agricultural produce, the rate varies based on irrigation. Crops irrigated by rain, rivers, or springs without human effort owe one-tenth (ushr) of their yield. Crops that require human irrigation with cost and effort owe one-twentieth (nisf al-ushr). Al-Shafi'i's nisab for agricultural produce is five wasqs, equivalent to approximately 653 kilograms of grain. He applies zakat to the main staple crops — wheat, barley, dates, and raisins — and to anything stored as food. This is narrower than the Hanafi view, which extends zakat to all agricultural produce.

Al-Shafi'i also addresses zakat on trade goods (amwal al-tijarah), requiring that they be valued at the end of the lunar year and zakat be paid at 2.5% of their market value if that value reaches the nisab of gold or silver. The trader's intention to hold goods for trade from the moment of acquisition is a condition for trade goods to be zakatable.

The Quran specifies eight categories of eligible zakat recipients: the poor (fuqara'), those in need (masakin), those employed to collect zakat, those whose hearts are to be reconciled (mu'allafat al-qulub), those in bondage seeking to free themselves, those in debt, those striving in the way of Allah, and travelers in need. Al-Shafi'i holds that zakat must be distributed among all eight categories when they are present, or among whichever categories exist in the locality. He does not permit restricting zakat to a single category, a position that differs from the Maliki and Hanbali schools, which give the payer more discretion.

For zakat al-fitr, the charitable payment due at the end of Ramadan, al-Shafi'i holds that it is obligatory on every Muslim who possesses more than his basic needs and the needs of his dependents on the night of Eid. The amount is one sa' (approximately 2.6 kilograms) of the staple food of one's region — dates, wheat, barley, raisins, or their equivalent. Al-Shafi'i permits paying zakat al-fitr in wheat, unlike the majority Maliki view that restricts it to the food types mentioned in the Sunnah.
content_id:
Zakat, sedekah tahunan yang wajib dikeluarkan, adalah rukun Islam yang ketiga dan salah satu bentuk ibadah yang paling terperinci pengaturannya. Dalam Al-Umm, al-Syafi'i membahas zakat dengan ketelitian yang sama seperti yang beliau terapkan pada hadis-hadis.

Pembahasan zakat dalam Al-Umm mencakup: nisab dan haul emas, perak, dan uang; zakat perniagaan; zakat pertanian dan buah-buahan (kurma dan anggur) dengan nisab lima wasaq; zakat ternak (unta, sapi, kambing) beserta nisab dan kadar masing-masing; zakat rikaz (harta karun) dan ma'din (hasil tambang); zakat fitrah yang wajib di akhir Ramadan; delapan golongan yang berhak menerima zakat sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Al-Quran (QS 9:60); syarat-syarat wajib zakat; zakat kepada kerabat; dan distribusi zakat oleh amil. Al-Syafi'i juga membahas perbedaan pendapat dengan Imam Malik dan Abu Hanifah dalam beberapa masalah zakat.
content_ar:
الزكاة الركن الثالث من أركان الإسلام ومن أدق العبادات تنظيماً. ويُعالجها الشافعي في الأم بنفس الصرامة النصية التي يُعامل بها سائر أبواب العبادات: حكم يُستنبط من نص، وقياس يُقيَّد بضوابطه.

وفي نصاب الذهب والفضة يستند الشافعي إلى الأحاديث الواردة في مقاديرها ويُبيّن نسبة الزكاة في كل منهما. وفي عروض التجارة يُعلق الوجوب على بلوغ القيمة نصاب الفضة مع الحول، مُعللاً ذلك بأن الغرض من عروض التجارة النماء كالنقدين.

وفي زكاة الزروع والثمار يُفرّق الشافعي بين المسقيّ بالمطر والمسقيّ بآلة وأثر هذا التفريق في نسبة الزكاة. وفي الأنعام يُعرض جدول التقدير التفصيلي للإبل والبقر والغنم مع النصاب المقرر في السنة لكل منها.
